name: ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types
title: ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types
summary: Common protobuf types used in Google APIs
description:
Common protobuf types used in Google APIs
Google APIs are typically deployed as API services that are hosted under
different DNS names. One API service may implement multiple APIs and multiple
versions of the same API.
.
Google APIs use Protocol Buffers version 3 (proto3) as their Interface
Definition Language (IDL) to define the API interface and the structure of the
payload messages. The same interface definition is used for both REST and RPC
versions of the API, which can be accessed over different wire protocols.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types because Common protobuf types used in Google APIs.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-googleapis-common-protos-types --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
